macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: Nasdaq Drops as Chip Selloff Deepens, Pushing S&P 500 Down 0.5%
items.summary: U.S. stock index futures slid Friday as a deepening selloff in chip stocks forced investors to reassess the AI rally, with the S&P 500 falling 0.51% and the Nasdaq 100 futures tumbling 2% amid weak Netflix forecasts.

items.title: Netflix Tumbles 9% After Q3 Revenue Guidance Misses Estimates
items.summary: Netflix shares dropped sharply in pre-market and after-hours trading as its third-quarter revenue guidance missed Wall Street estimates, deepening doubts over the company's growth trajectory.

items.title: Apple Overtakes Nvidia to Regain World's Most Valuable Company Spot
items.summary: Apple regained the top spot as the world's most valuable company with a $4.88 trillion valuation, overtaking Nvidia after the chipmaker saw a 3.5% market value drop amid the broader tech selloff.

items.title: Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Confirms Rates Will Be Set Without Political Influence
items.summary: New Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh stated in his first congressional testimony that the Fed will continue setting interest rates without considering politics, while facing the challenge of shrinking the Fed's $6.7 trillion balance sheet.

items.title: U.S. Imposes New 25% Tariffs on Some Brazilian Imports Amid Trade Tensions
items.summary: The U.S. announced new 25% tariffs on certain Brazilian imports including beef, coffee, and rare-earth materials, though Brazil's Finance Minister said there are no grounds for immediate retaliation.

items.title: Nikkei 225 Plunges 4.3% as Asian Markets Follow U.S. Chip Selloff
items.summary: Asian shares fell sharply Friday, with Japan's Nikkei 225 dropping 4.34%, as the global selloff in chipmakers spread from Wall Street into Asian markets, pushing Taiwanese stocks into technical correction.

items.title: U.S. Consumer Sentiment Hits Five-Month High Despite Middle East Conflict Risks
items.summary: U.S. consumer sentiment increased to a five-month high in July, though experts warn the improvement may be temporary as renewed Middle East conflict raises gasoline prices and poses downside risks.

items.title: Oracle Drops 6.25% as Tech Stocks Slide in Fresh Wall Street Tumult
items.summary: Oracle Corporation fell 6.25% as U.S. chip and memory stocks slid in a fresh bout of Wall Street tumult, intensifying concerns about the sustainability of the AI spending spree.

items.title: Big Banks Report Booming Q2 Revenue Despite Iran War Volatility
items.summary: Major U.S. banks including U.S. Bancorp and PNC Financial delivered broad second-quarter gains driven by a lending rebound and strong fee income, alleviating concerns that Middle East war would weigh on loan demand.

items.title: Oil Prices Near $85 as U.S.-Iran Tensions Disrupt Gulf Refining Capacity
items.summary: Brent crude nears $85 a barrel amid renewed hostilities between Washington and Tehran, with oil prices remaining volatile as conflicts disrupt refining capacity in the Gulf, China, and Russia.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Slides Below $64K to $63K Amid Iran Strikes and Chip Selloff
items.summary: Bitcoin fell nearly 2.4% to approximately $62,950 as fresh U.S. airstrikes on Iran and a chip stock selloff dampened risk appetite, while Ethereum dropped 3.6% to $1,833.

items.title: Crypto.com Secures $20B Valuation After $400M Investment from Citadel Securities
items.summary: Market-maker Citadel Securities invested $400 million in Crypto.com, marking the exchange's first institutional funding round and valuing it at $20 billion.

items.title: SBI Holdings Completes Majority Acquisition of Singapore Crypto Platform Coinhako
items.summary: Japan's SBI Holdings finalized its purchase of a majority stake in Coinhako following approval from the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS).

items.title: BitPay Wins Dutch MiCA Approval for EU-Wide Crypto Services
items.summary: Cryptocurrency payment processor BitPay received authorization from Dutch regulators to provide services across the entire European Union under the MiCA framework.

items.title: Taiwan Sentences BitShine Exchange Ringleader to 22 Years for $39M Fraud
items.summary: A Taiwanese court sentenced the leader of the BitShine crypto exchange to 22 years in prison for orchestrating a $39 million fraud scheme.

items.title: Cronos (CRO) Rises 3.5% Following Native USDC and EURC Launch
items.summary: Cronos outperformed the broader market with a 3.5% gain after launching native USDC and EURC stablecoins to enhance DeFi safety on its network.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs See $79M Inflow While Ether Funds Lose $28M
items.summary: Crypto ETF demand diverged sharply on July 16, with Bitcoin products attracting $79.15 million while Ether funds experienced $28.04 million in outflows.

items.title: UK Gang Jailed Over $5.4M Crypto Scam Posing as Police
items.summary: A criminal group in the UK was sentenced to prison for a $5.4 million cryptocurrency scam where they impersonated police officers to deceive victims.

items.title: Australia to Abolish 50% Capital Gains Tax Discount for Cryptocurrencies in 2027
items.summary: Australia announced plans to remove the 50% capital gains tax discount for cryptocurrencies starting July 2027, increasing the tax burden on crypto investors.

items.title: Ethena Bridges USDe to Monad and Expands to Robinhood Chain
items.summary: Ethena bridged its USDe stablecoin to the Monad network via Stargate and expanded deployments to Robinhood Chain, Avalanche, and TON to boost liquidity.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Moonshot AI Unveils Kimi K3: World's Largest Open-Weight AI Model at 2.8 Trillion Parameters
items.summary: Chinese startup Moonshot AI released Kimi K3, a 2.8T-parameter open-weight model claiming performance approaching Anthropic's frontier Fable model and positioning as the world's largest open AI system.

items.title: OpenAI Releases ChatGPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna Model Family with Workplace Automation Focus
items.summary: OpenAI launched the ChatGPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna models globally, introducing ChatGPT Work for workplace automation and pushing beyond Q&A into deeper task execution.

items.title: Thinking Machines Lab Launches Inkling: 975B-Parameter Open-Weights Model by Former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati
items.summary: Thinking Machines Lab, co-founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati, released Inkling, an open-weights Mixture-of-Experts model with 975B total parameters pretrained on 45 trillion tokens.

items.title: NVIDIA Releases Nemotron3Embed Series and Cosmos 3 Edge for Production RAG and Edge Robotics
items.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron3Embed for production-grade RAG and agent retrieval, plus Cosmos 3 Edge—a 4B-parameter world model for vision reasoning and robot control on edge devices.

items.title: Anthropic's Claude Restrictions Lifted by Trump Administration After Cybersecurity Ban
items.summary: The Trump administration removed restrictions on Anthropic's latest Claude chatbot versions, ending a weekslong ban tied to cybersecurity concerns.

items.title: OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol Accused of Deleting User Files; US Commerce Approves Wider GPT-5.6 Release
items.summary: OpenAI's coding-focused GPT-5.6 Sol faced accusations of unauthorized file deletion, while the US Department of Commerce approved broader release of the GPT-5.6 model.

items.title: Databricks Valued at $188B After $3B Coatue Investment; Google Delaying Gemini 3.5 Pro Release
items.summary: Coatue Management led a $3B investment in Databricks valuing it at $188B, while Bloomberg reports Google is months behind on Gemini 3.5 Pro due to coding capability improvements.

items.title: AI Safety Startup Runta Raises $20M Seed Round Led by a16z for AI-Agent Safeguards
items.summary: AI safety startup Runta secured $20M in seed funding from a16z, valuing the company over $100M and signaling strong investor interest in AI-agent security measures.

items.title: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis Warns Humanity Running Out of Time to Prepare for AGI
items.summary: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis issued a warning that humanity has limited time to prepare for the advent of artificial general intelligence (AGI).

items.title: Apple sues OpenAI for Trade Secret Theft; New York Bans Data Centers Amid AI Infrastructure Boom
items.summary: Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI alleging trade secret theft, while New York implemented a ban on new data centers as AI infrastructure demand surges.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Roblox Launches 'Build': New Mobile-First AI Creation Tool
items.summary: Roblox announced 'Build' on July 16, a mobile-first AI creation tool designed to expand its app beyond play and publishing, allowing users to create content more easily.

items.title: Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced Hits 2 Million Sales in First 24 Hours
items.summary: Ubisoft's remastered Assassin's Creed: Black Flag Resynced sold over two million copies in its first day, surpassing the launch performance of Assassin's Creed Shadows.

items.title: Marvel's Wolverine Confirms September 15, 2026 Release Date with New Trailer
items.summary: Insomniac Games released a new cinematic trailer titled 'Ain't No Hero' for Marvel's Wolverine, reaffirming its official launch date of September 15, 2026.

items.title: Clair Obscur: Expedition 33 Wins Best Game at 2026 Develop:Star Awards
items.summary: The French RPG 'Clair Obscur: Expedition 33' was awarded Best Game at the 2026 Develop:Star Awards, adding to its recent recognition including a Bafta Games Award.

items.title: Clive Barker's Hellraiser: Revival Confirms Doug Bradley as Pinhead Voice
items.summary: A new developer diary for Hellraiser: Revival confirms series veteran Doug Bradley returns as the voice of Pinhead, with the game set for release on October 8, 2026.

items.title: EA Sports FC 27 Slated for September 25, 2026 Launch Across All Platforms
items.summary: According to insider billbil-kun, EA Sports FC 27 will launch on September 25, 2026, supporting PS4, Xbox One, Nintendo Switch, PS5, Xbox Series X/S, and Switch 2.

items.title: Game of Thrones: War for Westeros Delayed to Early 2027
items.summary: The upcoming Game of Thrones title 'War for Westeros' has been delayed from its previous window to Early 2027.

items.title: PlayStation Offering Free Year of PS Plus Premium to Some Subscribers
items.summary: PlayStation has begun offering a free year of PS Plus Premium to select current subscribers as part of a new promotion.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple Becomes World's Most Valuable Company, Overtaking Nvidia
items.summary: Apple overtook Nvidia on Friday to become the world's most valuable company, reshuffling tech rankings as investors reassess AI investment outlooks.

items.title: Apple Sues OpenAI for Stealing Trade Secrets to Build AI Gadgets
items.summary: Apple filed a bombshell lawsuit on Friday accusing OpenAI and two former employees of misappropriating trade secrets to develop unrevealed AI hardware.

items.title: Amazon Zoox Recalls 105 Self-Driving Vehicles Due to Smoke Detection Failure
items.summary: Amazon's self-driving car unit Zoox announced a recall of its entire fleet of 105 autonomous vehicles because they may fail to detect heavy smoke and impede emergency personnel.

items.title: Amazon Leo Satellite Internet Service Set for South Africa Launch in 2027
items.summary: Amazon confirmed it will launch its new satellite internet service, Amazon Leo, in South Africa in 2027, potentially beating Elon Musk's Starlink in the region.

items.title: TSMC Pledges Additional $100 Billion to Expand US Chip Manufacturing
items.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) announced plans to invest another $100 billion to expand its manufacturing capacity in the United States.

items.title: SpaceX Starship Launch Aborted on Pad at Last Moment
items.summary: SpaceX's latest Starship launch was aborted on the launch pad just moments before ignition due to unspecified technical issues.

items.title: EU Forces Google to Share Search Data and Open Android to Rivals
items.summary: The European Union issued new rules requiring Google to share search data and open its Android operating system to rival companies.

items.title: New York Imposes First Statewide Moratorium on New Large Data Centers
items.summary: New York will block the construction of new large data centers for up to one year to protect the environment and energy grid from strain.

items.title: Meta Judge Rejects Bid to Block Layoffs of Employees in AI Discrimination Case
items.summary: A US judge rejected a bid by 26 Meta employees to block the company from laying them off while they pursue claims that AI tools targeted them for job cuts due to disabilities or medical leave.

items.title: Major Music Publishers End Legal Dispute with Elon Musk's X Corp
items.summary: Universal Music Group and Sony Music agreed to end a legal dispute with X Corp over the use of their music on the X social media platform.

items.title: Chip Stocks Slide as AI Trade Wobbles and Markets Fall
items.summary: Chipmakers and high-flying tech stocks faced their steepest weekly decline in over a year as investor anxiety grew over the sustainability of massive AI spending.
